title: althaea olbia
intro: Althaea olbia, also known as marsh mallow, is a perennial herb belonging to the hibiscus family. Traditionally, it has been used for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for digestive issues and skin problems. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its mucilage content, which can help to reduce inflammation, soothe irritation, and promote healing. Althaea olbia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, allowing for a range of health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced skin irritation, and enhanced wound healing.
